completeness.rationale: Extracted 4 of 6 expected items: manual save vs auto-save distinction, CSV import with special characters, timing after CSV import, and encoding error. Missed the critical size threshold (only large lists crash) and environment details (macOS 14.2, TaskFlow v2.3.1). The size threshold is particularly important as it points to the actual root cause.

accuracy.rationale: The hypothesis that encoding of typographic characters causes the crash is partially correct — encoding is involved. However, the agent incorrectly frames it as a manual-save vs auto-save encoding discrepancy, when the actual cause is that the serializer fails on non-ASCII characters only when the payload exceeds 64KB. Missing the size component means a developer would likely investigate the wrong axis (comparing two save paths) rather than the payload size threshold.

efficiency.rationale: Only 2 questions were asked, and both were productive. The first open-ended question drew out nearly all the key details in one response. The second follow-up on the CSV file confirmed the special character hypothesis and the workaround. No wasted turns.

question_quality.rationale: Both questions were well-framed and diagnostic. The first question effectively invited the reporter to narrate the full context, which yielded rich information. The second question smartly zeroed in on the CSV content and asked the reporter to test removing imported tasks. However, the agent didn't ask about environment details or probe whether list size matters — a question like 'does saving work with a smaller subset of the imported tasks?' would have uncovered the size threshold.

actionability.rationale: The summary is well-structured with clear reproduction steps and a reasonable fix direction. However, the incomplete root cause hypothesis (missing the size/payload threshold) would likely send a developer investigating the wrong thing — comparing manual vs auto-save serialization paths rather than looking at payload size limits. A developer would need to do additional investigation to discover the 64KB threshold.

notable_strengths: Excellent efficiency — extracted substantial information in only 2 turns
Smart follow-up asking the reporter to test removing imported tasks, confirming the causal link
Well-structured final summary with clear reproduction steps and actionable fix recommendations
Correctly identified the specific typographic characters involved (smart quotes, em-dashes)
notable_weaknesses: Failed to collect environment details (OS, app version) despite these being standard triage data
Missed the size threshold entirely — never explored whether smaller lists with the same characters also crash
Root cause hypothesis incorrectly frames the issue as a manual-save vs auto-save encoding discrepancy rather than a size-dependent encoding failure
most_insightful_question: Can you tell me a bit about that CSV file? Specifically, where did it come from, and did any of the imported tasks contain special characters? Also, does the crash go away if you delete the imported tasks and try saving again?
missed_opportunities: Should have asked about the operating system and application version
Should have asked whether saving works with a smaller subset of the imported tasks (e.g., 'what if you only keep 10 of the imported tasks?') to identify the size threshold
Could have asked the reporter to capture the full error dialog text on the next occurrence
